title: T-Mobile Has Plenty to Gain From a $260 Billion German Merger
summary: You want European champions? Try this for size. Deutsche Telekom AG is considering taking full control of T-Mobile US Inc. in a move that could create a $260 billion transatlantic telecoms giant. It’s easy to see why the German suitor wants to go from being T-Mobile’s lead shareholder to its outright owner. The benefits for T-Mobile are less obvious — until you consider its future without a deal.

title: Oil Rises on Elusive Peace Deal Outlook, Iran Gunboat Attacks
summary: Oil rose as peace negotiations between the US and Iran stalled, with both sides using blockades of the vital Strait of Hormuz to try and gain leverage in a seven-week war that has upended global energy markets.

title: US Stocks On Track For Record Close as Trump Extends Iran Truce
summary: US stocks headed for a fresh all-time closing high on Wednesday as President Donald Trump’s ceasefire extension with Iran boosted optimism among traders.

title: KKR Is Said to Invest $1.5 Billion in Tower Firm Vertical Bridge
summary: KKR & Co. is investing $1.5 billion in Vertical Bridge REIT LLC in a deal that values the tower operator at $10 billion to $15 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Jay Leno Hypes Up Burbank Airport Bond Sale to Muni Investors
summary: Jay Leno’s latest hosting gig involves a classic car, an airport construction site and municipal bonds.

title: Fair Isaac Shares Slump as Pulte Moves to Cut Credit Score Costs
summary: Shares of Fair Isaac Corp., known as FICO, sank on Wednesday, after the US government took steps that officials said were aimed at cutting costs for credit scores and making homeownership accessible to a wider swath of buyers.

title: Trump’s Shopping Spree for Equity Stakes Nears $21B
summary: Bloomberg's Scarlet Fu joins Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals." The Trump administration’s $20.9 billion buying spree for equity stakes in private-sector companies is the biggest such push into strategic industries since the Second World War, according to research by the Council on Foreign Relations. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trump Media CEO Exit Caps a 90% Drop as MAGA Stocks Stumble
summary: Truth Social has rarely been more important on Wall Street, where traders hang on White House posts that can send the market tumbling or soaring back up again. The company’s stock, though, is much less in vogue.

title: ECB’s Lane Sees ‘Natural’ Fiscal Case for Europe Common Debt
summary: European Central Bank Chief Economist Philip Lane said there’s a logical fiscal case for the region’s nations to issue joint bonds, though such a move would need to be underpinned by trust.

title: US Could Own Up to 90% of Spirit
summary: Bloomberg's Siddharth Philip joins Dani Burger and Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Deals." The Trump administration is nearing a rescue package for Spirit Airlines that could give the US government the option to own as much as 90% of the carrier once it emerges from bankruptcy, according to people familiar with the matter.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: 'Early Innings' of US Rare-Earth Independence: Humpton
summary: Barbara Humpton, CEO of USA Rare Earth, joins Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals." USA Rare Earth agreed to acquire Brazil’s Serra Verde Group in a $2.8 billion cash-and-stock transaction, marking one of the largest deals in the rare-earths industry. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Centerview’s Tony Kim Says AI Changes Pace, Structure of Deals
summary: The pace of advances in artificial intelligence is changing the structure of how deals are getting done in the space, according to Tony Kim, Centerview Partners’ co-president of investment banking.

title: S&P Near Peak as Chipmakers Rise 16th Straight Day: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks hovered near their all-time highs as a slew of strong earnings reports and President Donald Trump’s extension of a ceasefire with Iran revived risk appetites after a two-day retreat. Bitcoin rallied.

title: USA Rare Earth CEO Says US is in ‘Early Innings’ of Independence
summary: USA Rare Earth Inc.’s $2.8 billion acquisition of Brazil’s Serra Verde Group is a step toward US independence from China for rare earth metals, said Chief Executive Officer Barbara Humpton.

title: Petrobras Director Pans Low Fuel Prices, Exposing Board Rift
summary: A Petrobras board member appointed by non-controlling shareholders is warning that the state-controlled producer is losing billions from below-market fuel sales and risks reputational damage.

title: Mythos Breach Shows Need for AI Framework, Magaziner
summary:
Rep. Seth Magaziner (R) Rhode Island said Anthropic is doing the right think by warning the world about what they discovered about Mythos and working with the Pentagon, but that Congress needs to develop a federal framework for AI. 
A small group of unauthorized users have accessed Anthropic PBC’s new Mythos AI model, a technology that the company says is so powerful it can enable dangerous cyberattacks, according to a person familiar with the matter and documentation viewed by Bloomberg News.
A handful of users in a private online forum gained access to Mythos on the same day that Anthropic first announced a plan to release the model to a limited number of companies for testing purposes, said the person, who asked not to be named for fear of reprisal. The group has been using Mythos regularly since then, though not for cybersecurity purposes, said the person. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Bessent Says Gulf, Asian Allies Requested Currency Swap Lines
summary: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said Wednesday that many Persian Gulf allies along with a number of Asian nations have requested foreign exchange swap lines with the US, and pointed to the potential for such arrangements to support dollar-denominated lending overseas.

title: Texas Stock Exchange Expects First IPO in Early 2027
summary: Jeb Hensarling, the former US representative who is now an adviser to the Texas Stock Exchange, says the first initial public offerings on the startup bourse may come in 2027's first quarter. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Traders Return to Fed Funds Wager That Surged During Repo Stress
summary: Traders notched up record volumes in one narrow corner of the US interest-rate futures market this week, betting on a spread that reflects potential swings in overnight lending markets.

title: Trump Pushes $500 Million Plan to Save Spirit Airlines
summary: The Trump administration is nearing a rescue package for Spirit Airlines that could give the US government the option to own as much as 90% of the carrier once it emerges from bankruptcy. Bloomberg Intelligence Senior Aerospace Analyst George Ferguson discusses on Bloomberg Radio. (Corrects spelling of Spirit in headline)

title: Texas Stock Exchange Adviser Expects First IPOs in Early 2027
summary: The Texas Stock Exchange plans to host its first initial public offerings in the opening quarter of 2027 as it seeks to grab a chunk of business from Nasdaq and the New York Stock Exchange, said Jeb Hensarling, a strategic adviser to the Dallas-based marketplace.

title: Leftist Candidate in Copper Giant Peru Wants New Mining Rules
summary: A leftist presidential candidate on the verge of reaching Peru’s runoff election is pledging to overhaul mining rules in one of the world’s leading copper-exporting countries.

title: Trump’s Shopping Spree for Equity Stakes Nears $21 Billion
summary: The Trump administration’s $20.9 billion buying spree for equity stakes in private-sector companies is the biggest such push into strategic industries since the Second World War, according to research by the Council on Foreign Relations.

title: UBS Faces $20 Billion Hike in Capital Needs as Swiss Split Grows
summary: The Swiss government outlined plans that would add billions of dollars to UBS Group AG’s capital requirements, deepening a showdown with the lender over its proposed banking reforms.

title: Blackstone Credit Fund Set to Raise $850 Million From Bond Sale
summary: Blackstone Private Credit Fund has launched an $850 million investment-grade bond sale as private lenders known as business development companies ramp up debt issuance after a dry spell.

title: ECB’s Cipollone Not Worried About Delay of Digital Euro Vote
summary: European Central Bank Executive Board Piero Cipollone isn’t too worried about a potential delay of a key vote in the European Parliament on the digital euro project from early May to mid-June.

title: Yardeni, Who Pegged S&P 500 Nadir, Says War Risks Far From Over
summary: Ed Yardeni has watched market cycles long enough to know when panic is peaking.

title: Mark Cuban-Led Sports Fund Has $450 Million in Commitments
summary: A private equity fund focused on professional sports and led by Mark Cuban has secured $450 million in investor commitments at the first close of its flagship fund, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: BRB Shareholders Greenlight Capital Raise Up to $1.8 Billion
summary: Banco de Brasilia SA’s shareholders approved a capital raise of as much as 8.8 billion reais ($1.8 billion) to help bolster the Brazilian bank after it was stung by transactions it made with the failed Banco Master SA.

title: National Healthcare REIT Sinks 3.7% After $462 Million US IPO
summary: National Healthcare Properties Inc., slumped 3.7% after the real estate investment trust raised $462 million in an initial public offering priced below the marketed range.
